title: Divine Beast Dancing Lion
description: A legendary divine beast capable of controlling the elements of lightning, wind, and ice. Revered as a messenger of the heavens, its rage mirrors the tumult of the skies. When Messmer's army assailed Belurat, the Tower Settlement, the ritual of the lion dance was turned toward martial ends—its divinity, its fury, its light-footed beauty turned into a weapon of war.

title: Rellana, Twin Moon Knight
description: A former princess of the Carian Royal Family and the younger sister of Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. Rellana disavowed her birthright and chose to stand at Messmer's side, becoming known as the Sword of Messmer. She wields a pair of Light Greatswords imbued with Glintstone magic and Messmer's Flame, and is found at the end of Castle Ensis.

title: Messmer the Impaler
description: A demigod and the leader of the crusade against the Hornsent people, responsible for killing their Divine Beasts and burning the Tower Settlement. Revealed to be Queen Marika's son, secreted away and abandoned in the Land of Shadow. In combat, he utilizes Fire-based Spells and a Greatspear. His second phase, Base Serpent Messmer, reveals the Abyssal Serpent coiled within him.

title: Romina, Saint of the Bud
description: An amalgamation of various creatures—scorpion, centipede, butterfly, and humanoid. After her church was burned during Messmer's crusade, Romina discovered a twisted divine element and weaved it into the Scarlet Rot, wielding the bud she cradled as a deadly poleblade in combat. She is found in the Church of the Bud within the Ancient Ruins of Rauh.

title: Putrescent Knight
description: A large decrepit skeleton knight riding a ghostly steed, found guarding the entrance to St. Trina's cave at the end of Stone Coffin Fissure. All tainted flesh eventually becomes putrescence, and this clump of it imbibed St. Trina's nectar, which granted it eternal rest. And so it was that putrescence became her knight.

title: Commander Gaius
description: A towering commander who rides a boar into battle and uses several magic and gravity attacks. Gaius was in fact a warrior of albinauric extraction. Riding atop the boar he called his other half, both were as elder brothers to the lion, and both were cursed from birth. In spite of, or perhaps because of this very reason, Gaius was both Messmer's friend and the leader of his men.

title: Scadutree Avatar
description: The Realm of Shadow's equivalent of the Erdtree Avatar, resembling an enlarged Shadow Sunflower with two massive roots for arms. It can summon thorns and conjure the Scadutree's light for offense. The Scadutree is the shadow of the Erdtree—born of dark notions that bear no sense of Order, that twist and bend its stock, rendering it brittle. It is found at the Scadutree Base.

title: Metyr, Mother of Fingers
description: A towering celestial being composed of several fingers with two arms in front and a stomach made of smaller fingers. The mother of all Two Fingers and Fingercreepers, Metyr was in turn a magnificently gleaming daughter of the Greater Will, and the first shooting star to fall upon the Lands Between. She uses wide attacks and magic imbued with gravitational properties.

title: Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame
description: The embodiment of the Frenzied Flame, found as the final boss of Midra's Manse in the Abyssal Woods. As the golden barbs inflicted eternal agony upon him, Midra held fast to Nanaya's entreaty: 'Endure.' The word was a curse. He wields a barbed Greatsword infused with the Frenzied Flame and inflicts Madness upon those he strikes.

title: Bayle the Dread
description: A primeval legendary dragon and the forefather of the drakes, a sworn enemy of the ancient dragons, and the target of Igon's vengeance. Bayle turned upon Dragonlord Placidusax and they fought to a stalemate, leaving both severely wounded. He is found at the summit of the Jagged Peak, missing his left leg and most of his wings, and deals both Fire and Lightning damage.

title: Promised Consort Radahn
description: An imposing, fully armored humanoid enemy adorned in red and gold with a lion-shaped helmet, wielding two colossal swords. He is the final boss of Elden Ring: Shadow of the Erdtree. Radahn wields both Mohg's Bloodflame and his signature Gravity Magic. In his second phase, Radahn, Consort of Miquella, Miquella joins the fray, granting Radahn powerful Holy-based attacks.

title: Golden Hippopotamus
description: A towering foe with a head of a hippopotamus encountered within the Shadow Keep legacy dungeon. A formidable great enemy boss that guards the path deeper into the Shadow Keep, capable of devastating physical attacks and capable of impaling foes upon the great spines that erupt from its body.

title: Blackgaol Knight
description: A powerful knight clad in dark armor encountered in the Belurat Gaol of the Gravesite Plain. A formidable field boss armed with a greatsword and a crossbow, the Blackgaol Knight serves as one of the first major challenges awaiting those who enter the Land of Shadow.

title: Demi-Human Swordmaster Onze
description: A swift and deadly demi-human swordmaster encountered in the Gravesite Plain. Onze wields dual blades with remarkable speed and ferocity, presenting a dangerous challenge to unprepared Tarnished who venture into the Land of Shadow.

title: Ancient Dragon Senessax
description: A great ancient dragon capable of controlling the elements of lightning, wind, and fire. Found on the west side of the Jagged Peak, Senessax is a formidable great enemy that guards the paths of the jagged volcanic mountain range of the Land of Shadow.

title: Dancer of Ranah
description: A swift and deadly foe armed with two swords, found in the Southern Nameless Mausoleum along the Cerulean Coast. The Dancer of Ranah is a great enemy boss who fights with fluid, dance-like movements, wielding the Dancing Blade of Ranah with lethal grace.

title: Jori, Elder Inquisitor
description: A towering foe who holds the Barbed Staff-Spear and is found deep within the Abyssal Woods. Jori is a great enemy boss who served as an inquisitor in the Land of Shadow, using his imposing weapon with crushing force against any who trespass into the forbidden forest.
